steps.title: Fastest path: graph or solve the equation
steps.content:
Type the equation into Desmos as written:
$$(3x + 8)(4x - 3) = 0$$Graph or solve it directly and read the x-value that works. The requested value is $- \frac{8}{3}$.

steps.title: Apply the zero-product property
steps.content: The equation is already factored, so set each factor of $(3x+8)(4x-3)=0$ to zero: $3x+8=0$ gives $x=-\tfrac{8}{3}$, and $4x-3=0$ gives $x=\tfrac{3}{4}$.

steps.title: Identify the matching solution
steps.content: The solutions are $-\tfrac{8}{3}$ and $\tfrac{3}{4}$. That is the correct answer.
